title: T-136 — UI: FA detail page shell + tabs container (fa_detail prototype) NPD-a slice of T-020
prompt:
# T-136 — FA detail shell + tabs slice

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md §3, §5 + UX prototypes/design/01-NPD-UX.md fa_detail
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/fa-screens.jsx:300-401 (fa_detail)
Prototype-index entry: fa_detail
Parent root: T-020

## Goal
FA detail page shell at /(npd)/fa/[productCode] with a shadcn Tabs container exposing the 8 dept tab labels (Core/Planning/Commercial/Production/Technical/MRP/Procurement/History) in the prototype order. Active tab is URL-driven via ?tab=<slug>; each tab body is a placeholder shell (header + 'deferred-empty' state) — tab content lives in T-023..T-028. Browser back/forward restores the previously active tab.

## Implementation contract
1. apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/[productCode]/page.tsx (RSC) fetches FA core row + passes to the Client tabs container.
2. apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/[productCode]/_components/fa-tabs.tsx ('use client') renders shadcn Tabs with 8 TabsTrigger entries (Core/Planning/Commercial/Production/Technical/MRP/Procurement/History) in order; active tab is read from useSearchParams ?tab=<slug>; switching pushes new URL via router.
3. Each TabsContent is a placeholder Card with header label + 'Tab content deferred' state — actual content is filled in T-023..T-028.
4. No HTML role='tablist' — use shadcn Tabs primitives only.

## Files
- apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/[productCode]/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/[productCode]/_components/fa-tabs.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/[productCode]/_components/__tests__/fa-tabs.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the FA detail page renders, when compared to prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/fa-screens.jsx:300-401 (fa_detail), then it shows the same 8 tab labels in the same order (Core/Planning/Commercial/Production/Technical/MRP/Procurement/History) using shadcn Tabs+Card primitives (no HTML role='tablist') — verified by RTL counting tabs + parity checklist
2. Given ?tab=technical is in the URL, when the page renders, then the Technical TabsTrigger is active and the Technical placeholder body is shown — verified by RTL
3. Given a user switches tabs and uses browser back, when navigation occurs, then the previous tab is restored — verified by RTL router test

## Test strategy
- RED: fa-tabs.test.tsx asserting 8 tab labels + URL persistence + back-restore + primitive parity before component exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter web test fa-tabs'

## Risk red lines
- Do not use HTML role='tablist' — use shadcn Tabs primitives
- Do not include tab content (deferred to T-023..T-028)
- Do not skip URL persistence for ?tab=
